metrics.title: Q4 2025 Revenue
metrics.label: +110% YoY — FY2025 revenue $5.13B (+168% YoY); net loss -$1.20B

metrics.title: Revenue Backlog
metrics.label: Up from $66.8B at Q4 2025; Meta deal extension to 2032 + Vera Rubin commit

metrics.title: Customer Concentration
metrics.label: Microsoft 67% of FY2025 rev; OpenAI ~33% of contracted future rev; concentration easing in 2026

moat.description: Pure-play NVIDIA GPU cloud with first-mover scale, deep NVIDIA partnership, and earliest access to next-gen architectures — but heavily concentrated revenue and asset-financed business model limit moat durability.
moat.analysisSummary: CoreWeave's edge is **operational velocity and NVIDIA preferred-partner status**, not a structural moat:

analysisPoints.title: NVIDIA Preferred Partner
analysisPoints.text: CoreWeave is repeatedly first-to-market with new NVIDIA architectures (H100, H200, GB200, Vera Rubin) — a status reinforced by NVIDIA's equity stake. This deployment-velocity advantage compounds during platform transitions.

analysisPoints.title: Hyperscaler Customer Concentration
analysisPoints.text: Microsoft accounted for 67% of FY2025 revenue; OpenAI represents ~33% of contracted future revenue under an $11.9B five-year deal; Meta added a $21B contract through 2032. Three customers = bulk of book — both a strength (visibility) and weakness (renewal risk).

analysisPoints.title: Asset-Heavy Capital Model
analysisPoints.text: CoreWeave funds GPU buildouts with collateralized debt (~$21B as of late 2025) backed by long-term customer contracts. The model works while contracts are signed faster than depreciation runs — a fragile equilibrium that breaks if AI capex digests.

growth.description: FY2025 revenue of $5.13B (+168% YoY) and Q4 2025 of $1.57B (+110% YoY) reflect hypergrowth on the back of the NVIDIA platform ramp. Revenue backlog exploded to ~$95B (from $66.8B at Q4) on the Meta extension to 2032 and Vera Rubin commitments. FY2026 consensus revenue sits around $14–16B (~180–200% growth). However, GAAP losses widened to -$1.20B and the model remains cash-flow negative as capex outpaces revenue recognition. Q1 2026 print May 7 is the next catalyst.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base 95 (hypergrowth at scale: 168% FY2025, 110% Q4 2025, ~$95B backlog supporting multi-year visibility) − 5 customer concentration risk and unit-economics uncertainty as scale rises = 90

drivers.name: Hyperscaler Capacity Contracts
drivers.metric: $95B revenue backlog; Microsoft 67% FY2025 + OpenAI $11.9B + Meta $21B + smaller AI labs

drivers.name: NVIDIA Architecture Refresh
drivers.metric: First-to-market on Blackwell GB200; Vera Rubin commitments embedded in Meta 2032 contract

drivers.name: Sovereign + Enterprise AI
drivers.metric: Early traction with European and Middle East sovereign AI deployments; revenue mix still <10%

growthAnalysis.keyRisk: If Microsoft accelerates its in-house AI infrastructure buildout (Azure + AMD MI400 + custom Maia ASICs) and lets the CoreWeave contract decay at 2027 renewal — CRWV's revenue trajectory inverts and the $21B+ debt stack triggers covenant pressure as collateralized GPUs depreciate faster than backlog recognition

valuation.description: At ~$125, CRWV trades at ~5× FY2026 estimated revenue (~$14B) and ~13× FY2025 sales — a discount to NVIDIA's ~14× P/S but with vastly inferior unit economics, GAAP losses, ~$21B debt load, and concentrated customer book. The valuation has decompressed from 2025 highs (~$187) on AI bubble concerns. The $95B backlog provides a partial valuation floor, but the asset-financed model, customer concentration, and uncertain steady-state margins keep the score in the mid-50s. This is a high-conviction speculative bet, not a value name.

rows.label: Forward P/E (NTM)
rows.note: still loss-making; profitability uncertain in FY2026

rows.label: EV / Sales (NTM)
rows.note: $69B mkt cap + ~$21B debt / ~$14B FY2026 revenue est.

peAnalysis.summary: Traditional P/E is not meaningful — CRWV is GAAP loss-making with a cash-burn profile. The ~6.5× EV/NTM-Sales is roughly half of NVIDIA but reflects vastly inferior margin structure (CRWV is essentially a leveraged GPU rental business with hyperscaler concentration). EV/Backlog of ~0.95× provides a partial valuation anchor — investors are paying roughly $1 for $1 of contracted future revenue, which is not cheap given execution risk, contract-renewal risk, and the asset-financed depreciation curve.

bear.description: Microsoft accelerates in-house Azure AI infrastructure and lets CoreWeave contract decay at renewal; AI capex digestion delays new bookings; debt covenants tighten as GPU depreciation runs faster than backlog recognition.
points: Microsoft contracts (67% of FY2025 revenue) are not renewed at full scale post-2027 as Azure builds out internal AI capacity with Maia ASICs and AMD MI400 GPUs, removing $8B+ of run-rate revenue
AI capex digestion in H2 2026–H1 2027 stops new hyperscaler bookings; backlog stalls below $100B and the multiple compresses toward 3× forward sales as growth decelerates
Rising interest expense on the $21B+ debt stack combined with GPU depreciation accelerating ahead of revenue recognition triggers covenant renegotiation and equity dilution

base.description: FY2026 revenue lands at $14–15B (~180% growth); customer concentration eases as OpenAI and Meta ramp through 2026; NVIDIA Vera Rubin transition reinforces preferred-partner status; first GAAP profitability glimpsed late 2027.
points: FY2026 revenue lands at $14–15B with Microsoft share declining toward 50% as OpenAI ($11.9B over 5 years) and Meta ($21B through 2032) capacity ramps materially
Backlog grows to $110–125B by year-end on incremental sovereign and enterprise AI deals plus Vera Rubin capacity bookings, providing 8+ years of forward revenue visibility
Adjusted EBITDA margin reaches ~40% by Q4 2026 (vs. ~35% in 2025) as the GPU base scales and operating leverage kicks in, though GAAP profitability remains 12–18 months away

bull.description: Sovereign AI and enterprise deployments accelerate; CoreWeave wins disproportionate share of Vera Rubin capacity; customer concentration de-risks materially; multiple re-rates on path to GAAP profitability.
points: Sovereign AI infrastructure spending accelerates ($150B+ globally) and CoreWeave captures a disproportionate share of Western non-hyperscaler deployments, adding $15B+ to FY2027 backlog
Vera Rubin capacity at CoreWeave reaches 2× the pace of any rival (including hyperscaler in-house programs), entrenching the NVIDIA preferred-partner economic moat through 2028
GAAP profitability is achieved by Q2 2027, the multiple re-rates to ~9–10× EV/Sales on de-risked unit economics, and equity dilution risk fades as FCF inflects positive

learnedInterfaces.note: Not applicable — CoreWeave sells raw GPU cloud capacity to AI labs and hyperscalers, not consumer UI experiences.

businessLogic.note: Not applicable — CoreWeave's stack runs CUDA workloads on NVIDIA hardware; no proprietary business-logic moat.

publicDataAccess.note: Not applicable — CoreWeave does not derive moat from public data access.

talentScarcity.note: Datacenter operations and large-scale GPU cluster engineering are scarce skills, but the talent pool is growing rapidly and large hyperscalers compete aggressively for the same engineers.

bundling.note: CoreWeave bundles GPU compute + networking + managed Kubernetes for AI workloads, but customers can replicate the stack on AWS/Azure/GCP or build in-house — the bundle is convenience, not lock-in.

proprietaryData.note: Customer workloads are private; CoreWeave does not derive proprietary data moat from the compute it sells.

regulatoryLockIn.note: Not applicable — no regulatory protection; in fact, CoreWeave faces export-control exposure on GPU re-rentals to restricted geographies.

networkEffects.note: Not applicable — GPU compute is fungible across providers; no network effect between CoreWeave customers.

transactionEmbedding.note: Multi-year capacity contracts ($95B backlog) embed CoreWeave operationally for the contract life, but contract renewal is open competition with hyperscalers and other GPU clouds.

systemOfRecord.note: Not applicable — CoreWeave is not the system of record for any customer's AI workload metadata or training history.

tenMoats.verdict: CoreWeave's moat is narrow and time-bounded: NVIDIA preferred-partner status plus operational velocity advantage during AI hypergrowth. Most of the 10 moats are destroyed or weakened — the business is fundamentally a leveraged, concentrated GPU rental operation. The investment case rests on hypergrowth durability (90 score), not moat depth. Suitable as a speculative position sized to risk.
